decks.notes: Esper Vial is an aggressive deck centered around using Aether Vial to cheat creatures into play quickly and disrupt the opponent's strategy with cards like Force of Will and Swords to Plowshares. The deck also focuses on creating value through card advantage with Yorion, Sky Nomad and other creature abilities. The deck has a mix of control elements like Wasteland and disruption with cards like Meddling Mage and Opposition Agent. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures while maintaining control of the board.

The main focus of this Eldrazi Aggro deck is to ramp up quickly using lands like Eye of Ugin and Eldrazi Temple to put powerful Eldrazi creatures into play ahead of schedule. Chalice of the Void disrupts opponents' game plans, while Glaring Fleshraker and Thought-Knot Seer provide early aggression and disruption. The deck also utilizes mana acceleration from Lotus Petal and Ancient Tomb to cast threats like Reality Smasher. Wasteland and Wastescape Battlemage help to disrupt the opponent's mana base, while Cavern of Souls ensures the Eldrazi spells cannot be countered.

The deck revolves around a high-risk, high-reward strategy of quickly dumping your entire deck into the graveyard and winning the game in one explosive turn. By utilizing cards like Thoughtseize and Pact of Negation for disruption and protection, along with powerful combo pieces like Balustrade Spy and Undercity Informer, the goal is to fill the graveyard with key cards like Thassa's Oracle and Dread Return to secure a victory. The deck's success hinges on precise sequencing and timing, as well as the ability to navigate through disruption from the opponent.

The deck focuses on utilizing counterspells and disruption to control the game, while also utilizing card draw and filtering to find the necessary answers. It aims to tempo opponents by bouncing their threats or countering key spells, while also having ways to deal with a variety of threats such as creatures or artifacts. The deck utilizes a mix of permanent-based and non-permanent based answers to create a flexible control shell that can adapt to different board states and matchups.

This deck focuses on utilizing disruptive creatures and removal spells to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan, while also applying pressure through aggressive creature threats. The deck aims to use cards like Stoneforge Mystic and Aether Vial to efficiently deploy threats, while also utilizing cards like Skyclave Apparition and Solitude to deal with opposing threats. The addition of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion adds a powerful late-game advantage by flickering key cards for additional value. Overall, the deck aims to disrupt and pressure the opponent to secure victory.

The deck focuses on quickly putting powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Emrakul, the Aeons Torn into play using cards like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack. By getting these creatures onto the battlefield ahead of schedule, the deck aims to overpower the opponent with their high power and abilities. It also includes counter spells like Force of Will and Flusterstorm to protect its key combos. The deck is designed to efficiently and consistently execute its game plan of cheating big creatures into play and overwhelming the opponent.

The deck utilizes the Show and Tell combo strategy to quickly bring out powerful creatures like Emrakul and Atraxa. By using cards like Sneak Attack and Omniscience, the player can overwhelm their opponent with high-impact creatures without paying their mana cost. Protection spells like Force of Will and Flusterstorm help ensure the combo goes off smoothly, while cantrips like Brainstorm and Ponder help dig for key pieces. With the ability to cheat out massive threats early in the game, the deck aims to win quickly and decisively before the opponent can respond.

The main idea of this control deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan by utilizing counter spells like Force of Will and Daze, as well as disruptive cards like Stifle and Wasteland. It aims to control the board with removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Teferi, Time Raveler while also generating card advantage with cards like Ponder and Brainstorm. The deck also includes powerful planeswalkers like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student to help with card advantage and disruption. Overall, the strategy revolves around controlling the game and outlasting the opponent until victory.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly bring out powerful Eldrazi creatures and disrupt the opponent's game plan. By using cards like It That Heralds the End, Thought-Knot Seer, and Reality Smasher, the deck aims to apply pressure early and maintain board control. Key cards like Chalice of the Void and Wasteland are used to disrupt the opponent's strategy while cards like Eldrazi Temple and Lotus Petal help ramp up towards the big creatures. The deck also features Eldrazi Obligator for additional disruption and finishing potential.

The main concept of this Doomsday combo deck is to use cards like Dark Ritual, Lotus Petal, and Doomsday to set up a winning sequence of cards in your library, culminating in a successful Thassa's Oracle win condition. The deck also includes disruption elements like Thoughtseize and Veil of Summer to protect your combo pieces. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student can help dig for key combo pieces, while cards like Force of Will and Daze offer protection against opposing disruptions. Overall, the deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and win the game in a single turn.

The main idea of this deck is to control the game through disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Daze, while also putting pressure on the opponent with aggressive creatures like Nethergoyf and Murktide Regent. The deck aims to maintain tempo by utilizing cards like Brainstorm and Mishra's Bauble to dig for answers and efficiently deal with threats. With a mix of disruption, card advantage, and efficient threats, this deck aims to outpace the opponent and secure victories through a combination of strategy and aggression.

The main concept of this Eldrazi Aggro deck is to quickly summon powerful Eldrazi creatures onto the battlefield, taking advantage of mana acceleration from Eldrazi Temple, Lotus Petal, and Ancient Tomb. Utilizing disruptive cards like Thought-Knot Seer and Chalice of the Void, as well as aggressive and evasive creatures like Devourer of Destiny, Eldrazi Linebreaker, and Reality Smasher, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and efficient beatdown strategy. Additionally, cards like Glaring Fleshraker and Kozilek's Command provide added versatility and removal options for maintaining board control.

The deck focuses on playing powerful Eldrazi creatures such as Reality Smasher and Thought-Knot Seer as quickly as possible by utilizing mana acceleration like Ancient Tomb and Lotus Petal. Chalice of the Void is used to disrupt the opponent's spells, while Wasteland is used to disrupt their mana base. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive Eldrazi creatures and maintain control of the game with powerful removal spells like Kozilek's Command and It That Heralds the End.

This UWx Control deck focuses on disruption and card advantage to control the game. By utilizing counterspells like Force of Will and Stifle, along with removal like Swords to Plowshares and Prismatic Ending, the deck aims to efficiently deal with threats. Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provide card advantage and additional control options. The deck also utilizes Murktide Regent as a threat to close out the game. With a combination of disruption, card advantage, and threats, this deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver opponents in the Legacy format.

This deck focuses on aggressively controlling the tempo of the game by disrupting the opponent's plans with cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Will while utilizing efficient threats like Murktide Regent and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student to close out the game quickly. The inclusion of Wasteland and Daze helps to keep the opponent off balance, while cards like Fatal Push provide removal options. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while applying pressure to quickly secure a victory.

The main focus of this Dragon Stompy Legacy 1 vs 1 deck is to aggressively disrupt the opponent's mana base and control the board using powerful artifacts and disruptive creatures. By using cards like Chalice of the Void, Magus of the Moon, and Blood Moon, the deck aims to shut down the opponent's spells and limit their ability to cast spells. The deck also includes powerful creatures like Karn, the Great Creator and Broadside Bombardiers to apply pressure and close out the game quickly. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with disruptive threats and aggressive plays.
